seoTitle: How to Connect Typeform to Microsoft Excel
metaDescription: Learn how to automatically send Typeform responses to Microsoft Excel for reporting, lead tracking, and survey analysis.
intro: Manually transferring form data into Excel eats up time that could go toward analysis instead. This guide covers how to connect Typeform to Microsoft Excel, set up automated syncing, and keep your workbook updated whenever a new response comes in.

steps.heading: Open Typeform Integrations
steps.body: Go to your Typeform account's integration settings to find the Microsoft Excel connection option.

steps.heading: Connect Microsoft Excel
steps.body: Authenticate your Microsoft account and select the Excel workbook you want to sync responses to.

steps.heading: Configure field mapping and automation
steps.body: Match your form questions to the correct spreadsheet columns and enable automatic syncing for new submissions.

faq.question: Does Excel update automatically with new responses?
faq.answer: Yes, once the integration is configured, new Typeform submissions are added to the connected Excel workbook automatically.

faq.question: Can this integration help with lead management?
faq.answer: Yes, it's commonly used for lead management, customer feedback tracking, survey analysis, and general business reporting.

faq.question: Do I need Excel Online for this to work?
faq.answer: This kind of integration typically works with a cloud-based Excel workbook rather than a purely local file.

faq.question: What if I add new questions to my Typeform later?
faq.answer: You may need to update the field mapping so new questions correctly map to columns in your Excel workbook.
